prompt: Create an executive operating model titled 'Turning Ambiguity into Capability'. Show a sequence: Name the Ambiguity -> Separate Facts from Assumptions -> Assign Decision Ownership -> Define Acceptable Risk -> Create Feedback Loops -> Preserve Learning -> Repeatable Capability. Use a circular or compounding loop to show that each ambiguous situation improves future decision capacity. Use clear, restrained typography and no motivational clichés.
proof_boundaries: Do not imply ambiguity is always beneficial.
Do not present the six-step sequence as the only valid method.
Do not claim quantified executive-performance outcomes.
